headline.purpose: Section/page titles — rare, only 2 uses across the codebase.

label.purpose: The dominant size (text-xs) — table cells, badges, nav labels, metadata.

mono.purpose: Spec IDs, file paths, keyboard shortcuts — never prose.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Command palette, confirmation modals, fix-bolt dialog — true stacked overlays only.

shadows.name: ambient-light
shadows.purpose: Light-mode card surface separation only; dark mode uses border+tint instead.

motion.name: transition-standard
motion.purpose: Default hover/state transition on cards and nav.

motion.name: transition-colors
motion.purpose: Text/border color shifts on hover for links and nav items.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default call-to-action button — Signal Violet, used sparingly.
components.css: .ds-btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 0 16px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary { background: #7c3aed; color: #ffffff; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #8b5cf6; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #8b5cf6; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Neutral action button — dark-mode variant shown.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #262626; color: #e5e5e5; height: 40px; padding: 0 16px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #404040; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Text input, dark-mode variant — two-stage hover-then-focus affordance.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; height: 40px; padding: 0 12px; border-radius: 4px; border: 1px solid #404040; background: #0a0a0a; color: #ffffff; font-size: 14px; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.15s; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #7c8194; } .ds-input:hover { border-color: #737373; } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: #8b5cf6; }

components.name: Phase Card
components.description: Console-tile card for each SDLC phase, with a status dot and hover border-brighten (no shadow lift, dark mode).
components.html:
Design
Runtime contract + visual/technical design
components.css: .ds-card { position: relative; display: block; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #262626; background: rgba(23,23,23,0.4); padding: 16px; text-decoration: none; transition: border-color 0.15s, background-color 0.15s; } .ds-card:hover { border-color: rgba(139,92,246,0.4); background: rgba(38,38,38,0.6); } .ds-card-dot { position: absolute; top: 12px; right: 12px; width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 9999px; display: block; } .ds-card-dot-active { background: #a78bfa; } .ds-card-icon { display: grid; place-items: center; width: 32px; height: 32px; border-radius: 6px; background: #262626; margin-bottom: 12px; } .ds-card-title { color: #ffffff; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-card-desc { color: #7c8194; font-size: 12px; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Spec-ID / count badge — always monospace, per The Identifier-Is-Mono Rule.
components.css: .ds-badge { font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; font-size: 11px; color: #7c8194; background: #1b1b1c; border: 1px solid #262626; border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 6px; }

components.name: Phase Nav
components.description: Sticky 48px console strip — six phase tabs, active tab in Signal Violet with inline badge counts.
components.html: Design 3/8ImplementationTesting
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: stretch; height: 48px; border-bottom: 1px solid #262626; background: #1b1b1c; } .ds-nav-tab { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 0 16px; color: #7c8194; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; transition: color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s; } .ds-nav-tab:hover { color: #ddd6fe; } .ds-nav-tab-active { color: #a78bfa; border-bottom-color: #7c3aed; } .ds-nav-badge { font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; font-size: 11px; color: inherit; opacity: 0.8; }

narrative.overview:
Apex reads like an audit trail, not a product page. The interface exists to be inspected line by line — spec IDs in monospace, statuses as small dots and badges, phases as a console strip rather than a marketing nav. `text-xs` is the dominant type size across the codebase (488 uses against 315 `text-sm`), and that density is not an oversight: it's the tell of a tool built for someone cross-referencing a Gherkin scenario against a task pack, not scrolling a feed. Structure earns the trust; nothing here persuades.

Against that neutral, dense backdrop, Signal Violet is the one warm signal — active phase, active tab, AI-touched state, primary action. It appears sparingly enough that its presence *means something* every time it shows up. This directly enforces PRODUCT.md's positioning: spec-anchored traceability, not spec-anchored decoration.

The system explicitly rejects the generic SaaS dashboard look (gradient heroes, hero-metric tiles, cream/paper body backgrounds) and the enterprise-PM-tool skin (Jira-clone chrome) named in PRODUCT.md's anti-references. Apex sits above Taiga/Jira as the reasoning layer; it should never be mistaken for another ticket tracker.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-first (neutral-900/#121113/#1b1b1c base), with a fully-mirrored light mode swapped via .apex-sidebar-light / .apex-main-light override classes rather than a separate token set.
One accent color (violet) carries all active/selected/AI meaning; status color (emerald/amber/red) is reserved strictly for state, never decoration.
Monospace for anything that's an identifier — spec IDs, file paths, keyboard shortcuts — never for prose.
Flat by default: 1px borders and background-tint layering do the work shadows would do elsewhere; shadows appear only on true overlays.

rules.name: The One-Signal Rule
rules.body: Violet is the only color allowed to mean "this is active or AI-touched." Status colors (emerald/amber/red) mean state, never selection. Don't reach for a second brand hue to add visual interest — that's what the neutral ramp and monospace are for.

rules.name: The Identifier-Is-Mono Rule
rules.body: Any string that functions as an ID, path, or shortcut renders in monospace, unconditionally. If it's typed into a URL, a story-index.json, or a Gherkin tag, it's mono on screen too — the typography itself signals "this is machine-addressable."

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only as a response to true stacking (a modal/overlay), never as ambient decoration on a card or button. If a shadow doesn't correspond to something rendering above the page, remove it.

dos: Do keep violet as the only color that signals "active / selected / AI-touched" (The One-Signal Rule) — status colors stay semantic-only.
Do render every spec ID, file path, and keyboard shortcut in monospace (The Identifier-Is-Mono Rule).
Do keep depth flat-by-default; reserve shadows for genuine overlays (command palette, modals) per The Flat-by-Default Rule.
Do mirror dark/light via the existing .apex-sidebar-light / .apex-main-light override classes rather than introducing a parallel light-mode token set.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS marketing dashboard — no gradient hero, no hero-metric-tile template, no cream/paper body background (per PRODUCT.md's anti-references).
Don't let Apex's chrome converge toward looking like Taiga/Jira — density is fine, but Apex is the reasoning layer above the PM tool, not a reskin of it (per PRODUCT.md).

Don't use border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored side-stripe accent. SectionHeading and Callout in components/ui/primitives.tsx currently do this — flagged as a known violation to fix in a future /impeccable polish pass, not an accepted pattern to replicate elsewhere.
Don't introduce a second brand hue alongside violet "for visual interest" — variety comes from the neutral ramp and typography, not from more color.
Don't add ambient shadow to cards or buttons at rest; if it's not an overlay, it stays flat.
